title: VVC/H.266 Codec Mastery: From Theory to Implementation
description: Master next-generation VVC encoding, explore modern codec architectures, and implement efficient video compression pipelines using cutting-edge tools and reference implementations.

resources.title: JVET VVC Test Model (VTM) Reference Software
resources.description: Official overview site for Versatile Video Coding (VVC) and the VVC Test Model (VTM) reference software from the Joint Video Experts Team. Hosts VTM encoder/decoder, experimental JEM software, and Benchmark Set (BMS) tools.

resources.title: uvg266 - Open-source VVC/H.266 Encoder
resources.description: Open-source VVC/H.266 encoder licensed under 3-clause BSD, developed by University of Helsinki's Ultra Video Group. Still under active development with ongoing RD-quality and speed improvements.

resources.title: x266 - Next Generation VVC/H.266 Encoder by MulticoreWare
resources.description: Open-source VVC/H.266 compliant encoder built on top of x265, featuring HDR/WCG support, 360° video encoding, and SDR video. Developed for achieving twice the coding efficiency of HEVC.

resources.title: VVCEasy
resources.description: Ready-to-use prebuilt VVC (H.266) binaries and players for Windows, macOS, Linux, FreeBSD, Haiku, Android, and WebAssembly. Includes vvdec, vvenc, uvg266 encoders/decoders, and FFmpeg builds with VVC support.

resources.title: Video Processing Benchmarks and Research
resources.description: Comprehensive video codec benchmarking and research platform with ML-based optimization methods, stereoscopic quality assessment, and codec comparison studies. Includes research on x264/x265 preset optimization and video quality metrics analysis.

resources.title: libmpeg2
resources.description: A free library for decoding MPEG-1 and MPEG-2 video streams. It's lightweight and portable, used in several open source media players for MPEG-2 playback.

resources.title: JVET VVC Test Model (VTM) Reference Software
resources.description: Official overview site for Versatile Video Coding (VVC) and the VVC Test Model (VTM) reference software from the Joint Video Experts Team. Hosts VTM encoder/decoder, experimental JEM software, and Benchmark Set (BMS) tools.

resources.title: PyAV
resources.description: A Pythonic binding for FFmpeg’s libraries (Libav). PyAV allows Python programs to read, write, and manipulate video and audio data by leveraging the powerful FFmpeg backend.

milestones.title: VVC Fundamentals & Reference Implementation
milestones.description: Understand VVC codec design principles through official JVET documentation and the VTM reference model to establish theoretical foundations.

milestones.title: Practical VVC Encoding with Open-Source Encoders
milestones.description: Hands-on experience with uvg266 and x266 encoders, comparing performance, quality trade-offs, and real-world encoding workflows.

milestones.title: Performance Analysis & Benchmarking
milestones.description: Evaluate codec efficiency using benchmarking platforms, analyze encoding metrics, and optimize settings for your specific use cases.

milestones.title: Integration & Advanced Python Workflows
milestones.description: Build automated encoding pipelines using PyAV and Python bindings, integrating VVC into production video processing workflows.

milestones.title: Deployment & Hardware Acceleration
milestones.description: Deploy VVC solutions across platforms using ready-to-use binaries and explore GPU-accelerated encoding with modern hardware APIs.

prerequisites: Understanding of video compression basics (H.264/H.265 familiarity)
Comfortable with command-line tools and FFmpeg fundamentals
Basic Python or systems programming experience
learningObjectives: Understand VVC/H.266 codec architecture and improvements over HEVC
Operate both uvg266 and x266 encoders effectively
Benchmark and optimize encoding parameters for quality/speed trade-offs
Deploy VVC solutions with hardware acceleration support

title: Protocols & Transport: From Fundamentals to Low-Latency Streaming
description: Master video transport protocols including HLS, DASH, RTMP, and WebRTC. Progress from protocol basics to implementing adaptive bitrate streaming and peer-to-peer solutions.

resources.title: RFC 8216: HTTP Live Streaming (HLS)
resources.description: The IETF RFC that specifies HTTP Live Streaming (HLS), defining the playlist format (M3U8) and media segment structure for Apple's streaming protocol.

resources.title: DASH Industry Forum (DASH-IF)
resources.description: An industry consortium that promotes MPEG-DASH. The DASH-IF provides guidelines, reference players, and test vectors to ensure interoperability of DASH streaming.

resources.title: MPEG-DASH MPD Python Library
resources.description: A Python implementation of the MPEG-DASH schema and tools to validate or create MPD files. It aligns with the official XML schemas for DASH, helping generate standard-compliant manifests for testing or production.

resources.title: TSDuck
resources.description: An open source toolkit for MPEG transport streams. TSDuck provides a rich set of command-line utilities and a C++ library for analyzing, editing, generating, and broadcasting MPEG TS streams.

resources.title: Puffer (Stanford Live Streaming Research)
resources.description: A free live TV streaming research study by Stanford University that uses volunteer viewers to train and evaluate new adaptive streaming algorithms in real-world conditions.

resources.title: CTA WAVE Low Latency Service Guidelines
resources.description: Guidelines from the CTA WAVE (Web Application Video Ecosystem) project focusing on low-latency streaming service implementation, including Low-Latency DASH and HLS considerations.

resources.title: Apple Technical Note TN2224 - HLS Best Practices
resources.description: An Apple Technical Note detailing best practices for HTTP Live Streaming. It covers how to encode media, segment files, set up playlists, and deliver HLS streams efficiently.

resources.title: CMAF for Low Latency Streaming – Bitmovin
resources.description: A detailed blog explaining how CMAF enables low-latency streaming. Bitmovin covers the technical aspects of chunked CMAF, the changes required in players and CDNs, and results from tests using their encoder and player in low-latency mode.

resources.title: mediasoup
resources.description: A modern WebRTC SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) for Node.js that enables building multi-party video conferencing and streaming applications with low latency.

resources.title: WebTorrent
resources.description: A streaming torrent client for the web. WebTorrent uses WebRTC for peer-to-peer transport and allows in-browser video streaming from torrents without plugins.

resources.title: Dash Sequences GPAC Wiki
resources.description: The GPAC Wiki provides DASH sequences for conformance testing and validation.

resources.title: Mux HLS Streams
resources.description: Mux offers a set of test HLS streams, primarily used by hls.js and hosted by Mux, for testing and development.

resources.title: Apple HLS Tools
resources.description: Apple's HLS Tools assist developers in creating and validating HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) content for Apple devices.

milestones.title: Protocol Fundamentals & Standards
milestones.description: Learn HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) and MPEG-DASH standards. Study RFC 8216 and ISO/IEC 23009-1:2019 specifications.

milestones.title: Traditional Streaming Implementation
milestones.description: Implement RTMP and transport stream handling. Use Nginx-RTMP, TSDuck, and DVBlast for live streaming setup.

milestones.title: Low-Latency Streaming Techniques
milestones.description: Explore CMAF, adaptive bitrate algorithms, and HLS best practices. Debug streaming with Wireshark and validation tools.

milestones.title: WebRTC & Peer-to-Peer Transport
milestones.description: Build real-time video applications using Pion WebRTC, PeerJS, and mediasoup. Handle NAT traversal with TURN/STUN.

milestones.title: Advanced Integration & Optimization
milestones.description: Combine protocols in production systems. Optimize with monitoring, conformance testing, and adaptive bitrate tuning.

prerequisites: Understanding of video codecs and containers
learningObjectives: Master HLS and MPEG-DASH specifications and implementations
Design and deploy RTMP and transport stream workflows
Implement low-latency streaming with CMAF and optimization techniques
Build WebRTC peer-to-peer and SFU-based video applications
Debug protocol-level issues and validate streaming compliance
Choose appropriate protocols for different use cases

title: Cross-Platform Video Player Development
description: Master video player architecture and implementation across desktop, mobile, and web platforms through hands-on exploration of existing player frameworks and libraries.

resources.title: Light Alloy
resources.description: A lightweight, freeware media player for Windows known for its fast startup and support of all common video formats with configurable keyboard shortcuts.

resources.title: OMXPlayer
resources.description: A hardware-accelerated command-line video player for the Raspberry Pi that utilizes the Pi’s GPU decoding for smooth playback.

resources.title: GNOME Videos (Totem)
resources.description: The default video player for the GNOME desktop (formerly Totem). It provides simple playback for local and network video streams on Linux.

resources.title: JRiver Media Center
resources.description: A comprehensive media center application with high-quality video and audio playback, library management, and home theater features.

resources.title: Able Player
resources.description: A fully accessible, cross-browser HTML5 media player library that supports captions, audio descriptions, and sign language tracks, designed to be inclusive for users with disabilities.

resources.title: MarcinMoskala/VideoPlayView
resources.description: A custom Android view that integrates a video player with a loader and placeholder image, enabling quick embedding of video playback in Android apps.

resources.title: Swift-YouTube-Player
resources.description: A Swift library for embedding and controlling YouTube videos in iOS applications via a WKWebView.

resources.title: Enhancer for YouTube
resources.description: A browser extension that adds a variety of features to enhance the YouTube watching experience (ad control, playback speed, themes, etc.).

milestones.title: Desktop Player Fundamentals
milestones.description: Understand video playback engines and desktop player architecture by studying reference implementations and lightweight players.

milestones.title: Mobile Video Player Development
milestones.description: Learn Android and iOS video player integration patterns, custom views, and GPU-accelerated rendering for mobile platforms.

milestones.title: Web & Streaming Integration
milestones.description: Explore HTML5 media players, embedded video frameworks, and cross-browser compatibility for web-based video delivery.

milestones.title: Advanced Features & Optimization
milestones.description: Implement hardware acceleration, custom controls, accessibility features, and performance optimization across all platforms.

prerequisites: Basic understanding of video codecs and containers
Familiarity with at least one programming language
Knowledge of platform-specific development (Android, iOS, or web)
learningObjectives: Design and implement custom video players for multiple platforms
Integrate existing video player libraries effectively
Optimize video playback performance and implement hardware acceleration
Add advanced features like custom controls and accessibility support
Understand video streaming and media delivery patterns
